mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations. Lower cost of capital directly increases present value of future corporate cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.01% as of July 2008 reflects ongoing easing from crisis response
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. Elevated joblessness depresses aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.7% in July 2008, elevated from prior year lows
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite unemployment rising, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales combined with falling housing starts signals weakening consumer demand and construction activity. This joint contraction in consumption and investment reduces near-term GDP growth expectations, triggering negative equity returns within 20 trading days as forward earnings forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1.5% month-over-month AND HOUST falls > 5% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1066k units in June 2008, down sharply from 2006 peaks
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the correlation between the joint signal and forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals contraction in manufacturing output and capacity utilization. Reduced supply-side activity reflects weakening business investment and production expectations, leading to negative equity returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices cyclical earnings downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.84 in July 2008, showing early signs of contraction
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are positive in > 70% of instances when INDPRO declines by the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment signals labor market weakness and reduced household income. Lower employment reduces consumer purchasing power and confidence, depressing aggregate demand and corporate revenues. This mechanism produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market rapidly reprices earnings expectations downward upon employment data release.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137,615k in July 2008, beginning to show employment losses
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation combined with restrictive monetary policy (high FEDFUNDS) signals a monetary tightening regime that constrains liquidity and increases borrowing costs. This dual pressure reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates and compressed profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 219.18 in July 2008 reflecting elevated inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the joint signal shows no statistically significant predictive power (p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth signals weakening aggregate demand and economic activity. Lower GDP growth reduces corporate revenue and earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices equity risk premiums upward in response to recession risk.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 0.5% annualized over the most recent quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11700.6 billion in Q1 2008, showing early signs of slowdown
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are positive in > 70% of instances when GDP growth falls below the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in housing starts and declining real GDP signals a demand-side shock originating from the housing sector. This joint deterioration in construction and overall economic activity reduces equity valuations through lower earnings expectations and increased financial system stress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 8% month-over-month AND PAYEMS declines > 75 thousand persons month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Housing crisis deepening in mid-2008 with cascading labor market effects
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction signal fails to outperform individual component signals (Rank IC difference < 0.01) over any 2-year rolling window.
